identificationModule.briefTitle: Study Comparing 13-valent Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine With 7-valent Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Phase 3, Randomized, Active-Controlled, Double-blind Trial Evaluating the Safety, Tolerability and Immunologic Noninferiority of a 13-valent Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine Compared to a 7-valent Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine in Healthy Infants Given With Routine Pediatric Vaccinations in the United States

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The purpose of this study is to evaluate the safety, tolerability, and immunogenicity of the 13-valent pneumococcal vaccine (13vPnC) compare to the 7-valent pneumococcal vaccine (7vPnC) and to compare the immune response to concomitant vaccines administered with 13vPnC and 7vPnC.

interventions.name: 13 valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine
interventions.description: 1 single 0.5 mL dose together with a concomitant dose of Pediarix and ActHIB at the 2-, 4-, and 6-month visits and ProQuad, PedvaxHIB, and VAQTA at the 12-15 month visit.

interventions.name: 7vPnc pneumococcal conjugate vaccine
interventions.description: 1 single 0.5 mL dose together with a concomitant dose of Pediarix and ActHIB at the 2-, 4-, and 6-month visits and ProQuad, PedvaxHIB, and VAQTA at the 12-15 month visit.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Achieving Antibody Level ≥0.35 μg/mL in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the 3-Dose Infant Series
primaryOutcomes.description: Percentages of Participants achieving World Health Organization (WHO) predefined antibody threshold ≥0.35 μg/mL along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after the 3-dose infant series (7 months of age)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Antibody Concentration in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group 1 Month After the Toddler Dose
primaryOutcomes.description: Antibody concentration/geometric mean concentration as measured by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) for 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Achieving Predefined Antibody Levels for Haemophilus Influenzae Type b, Diphtheria Toxoid, and Pertussis Antigens in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the Infant Series
primaryOutcomes.description: Predefined Antibody Levels for Haemophilus Influenzae Type b (\[Hib\] 0.15 µg/mL or 1.0 µg/mL), Diphtheria Toxoid (0.1 International Units \[IU\]/mL), and Pertussis antigens (Pertussis filamentous hemagglutinin \[FHA\] 40.5 Elisa Units \[EU\]/mL, Pertussis toxoid \[PT\] 16.5 EU/mL, Pertussis pertactin \[PRN\] 26 EU/mL).
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One Month After the Infant Series (7 months of age)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-specified Systemic Events
primaryOutcomes.description: Systemic events (any fever \[Fv\] ≥ 38 degrees Celsius \[C\], decreased (decr.) appetite, irritability, increased (incr.) sleep, decreased sleep, and hives \[urticaria\], use of antipyretic medication \[med\] to treat or prevent symptoms \[sx\]) were reported using an electronic diary. Participants may be represented in more than 1 category.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-specified Local Reactions
primaryOutcomes.description: Local reactions were reported using an electronic diary. Tenderness was scaled as Any (tenderness present); Significant (\[Sig.\], present and interfered with limb movement). Redness and swelling were scaled as Any (redness or swelling present); Mild (0.5 centimeters \[cm\] to 2.0 cm); Moderate (\[Mod.\], 2.5 to 7.0 cm); Severe (\[Sev.\], \> 7.0 cm). Participants may have been represented in more than 1 category.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Achieving Predefined Antibody Levels for Concomitant Vaccine Antigens Induced by Measles, Mumps, Rubella, Varicella (MMR-V) and Haemophilus Influenzae Type b (Hib)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after toddler dose (13 to 16 months of age)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Antibody Concentration of Hib PRP in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the Toddler Dose
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: one month after the toddler dose

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Antibody Concentration of Measles, Mumps, and Varicella ELISA in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the Toddler Dose
secondaryOutcomes.description: Normalization was performed for unit of measure "index value" as Index Value of 1.00 = 10 mIU/mL.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: one month after the toddler dose

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Antibody Concentration of Rubella in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the Toddler Dose
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: one month after the toddler dose

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Achieving Functional Antibody Titer ≥1:8 as Measured by Opsonophagocytic Activity Assay (OPA) in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group the 3-Dose Infant Series and the Toddler Dose
secondaryOutcomes.description: Percentage of participants achieving functional antibody titer ≥1:8 as measured by opsonophagocytic activity assay (OPA) along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: One month after infant series and one month after toddler dose

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Titer (GMT) as Measured by Opsonophagocytic Activity Assay (OPA) in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the 3-Dose Infant Series and the Toddler Dose
secondaryOutcomes.description: Geometric mean titer (GMT) as measured by opsonophagocytic activity assay (OPA) for 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: one month after the infant series and the toddler dose

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Healthy 2-month-old infants.
* Available for the entire study period.

Exclusion criteria:

* Previous vaccination with any vaccine before the start of the study.
* Known contraindication to vaccination.

overallOfficials.name: Medical Monitor
overallOfficials.affiliation: Wyeth is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer

references.citation: Bryant KA, Gurtman A, Girgenti D, Reisinger K, Johnson A, Pride MW, Patterson S, Devlin C, Gruber WC, Emini EA, Scott DA. Antibody responses to routine pediatric vaccines administered with 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine. Pediatr Infect Dis J. 2013 Apr;32(4):383-8. doi: 10.1097/INF.0b013e318279e9a9.

Yeh SH, Gurtman A, Hurley DC, Block SL, Schwartz RH, Patterson S, Jansen KU, Love J, Gruber WC, Emini EA, Scott DA; 004 Study Group. Immunogenicity and safety of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine in infants and toddlers. Pediatrics. 2010 Sep;126(3):e493-505. doi: 10.1542/peds.2009-3027. Epub 2010 Aug 23.

participantFlowModule.preAssignmentDetails: Participants were enrolled into the study according to inclusion/exclusion criteria without a screening period.
participantFlowModule.recruitmentDetails: Participants were recruited in the United States from September 2006 to January 2008.

groups.title: 13vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with measles, mumps, rubella varicella vaccine live (ProQuad), Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Meningococcal Protein Conjugate) (PedvaxHIB), and Hepatitis A Vaccine, Inactivated (VAQTA) at 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (7vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with measles, mumps, rubella varicella vaccine live (ProQuad), Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Meningococcal Protein Conjugate) (PedvaxHIB), and Hepatitis A Vaccine, Inactivated (VAQTA) at 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

achievements.comment: One participant was prerandomized but not consented.

groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (7vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). groups.title: Total
groups.description: Total of all reporting groups

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Achieving Antibody Level ≥0.35 μg/mL in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the 3-Dose Infant Series
outcomeMeasures.description: Percentages of Participants achieving World Health Organization (WHO) predefined antibody threshold ≥0.35 μg/mL along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Evaluable immunogenicity (per protocol) population consisting of participants who adhered to the protocol requirements, had valid and determinate assay results, and had no other major protocol violations; (n) = number of participants with a determinate IgG antibody concentration to the given serotype.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: One month after the 3-dose infant series (7 months of age)

groups.title: 13vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with measles, mumps, rubella varicella vaccine live (ProQuad), Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Meningococcal Protein Conjugate) (PedvaxHIB), and Hepatitis A Vaccine, Inactivated (VAQTA) at 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (7vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with measles, mumps, rubella varicella vaccine live (ProQuad), Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Meningococcal Protein Conjugate) (PedvaxHIB), and Hepatitis A Vaccine, Inactivated (VAQTA) at 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 4 the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 6B the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 9V the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 14 the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 18C the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 19F the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 23F the difference in percentages between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups \> -10%.

outcomeMeasures.title: Geometric Mean Antibody Concentration in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group 1 Month After the Toddler Dose
outcomeMeasures.description: Antibody concentration/geometric mean concentration as measured by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) for 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Evaluable immunogenicity (per protocol) population of eligible participants, had valid and determinate assay results, and had no other major protocol violations; (n) = number of participants with a determinate antibody concentration for the specified serotype.

groups.title: 13vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with measles, mumps, rubella varicella vaccine live (ProQuad), Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Meningococcal Protein Conjugate) (PedvaxHIB), and Hepatitis A Vaccine, Inactivated (VAQTA) at 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (7vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with measles, mumps, rubella varicella vaccine live (ProQuad), Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Meningococcal Protein Conjugate) (PedvaxHIB), and Hepatitis A Vaccine, Inactivated (VAQTA) at 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 4 the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) \> 0.5 (2-fold criterion).

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 6B the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) \> 0.5 (2-fold criterion).

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 9V the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) \> 0.5 (2-fold criterion).

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 14 the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) \> 0.5 (2-fold criterion).

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 18C the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) \> 0.5 (2-fold criterion).

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 19F the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) \> 0.5 (2-fold criterion).

analyses.groupDescription: For serotype 23F the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for each common serotype was declared if the lower limit of the 2-sided 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) \> 0.5 (2-fold criterion).

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Achieving Predefined Antibody Levels for Haemophilus Influenzae Type b, Diphtheria Toxoid, and Pertussis Antigens in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the Infant Series
outcomeMeasures.description: Predefined Antibody Levels for Haemophilus Influenzae Type b (\[Hib\] 0.15 µg/mL or 1.0 µg/mL), Diphtheria Toxoid (0.1 International Units \[IU\]/mL), and Pertussis antigens (Pertussis filamentous hemagglutinin \[FHA\] 40.5 Elisa Units \[EU\]/mL, Pertussis toxoid \[PT\] 16.5 EU/mL, Pertussis pertactin \[PRN\] 26 EU/mL).
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Evaluable immunogenicity (per protocol) population who adhered to the protocol requirements, had valid and determinate assay results, and had no other major protocol violations. (n) = number of participants with a determinate postinfant series antibody concentration to the given concomitant antigen.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: One Month After the Infant Series (7 months of age)

groups.title: 13vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). groups.title: 7vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (7vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with measles, mumps, rubella varicella vaccine live (ProQuad), Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Meningococcal Protein Conjugate) (PedvaxHIB), and Hepatitis A Vaccine, Inactivated (VAQTA) at 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

analyses.groupDescription: For Haemophilus influenzae type b the difference in percentage between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at 0.15μg/mL threshold was calculated.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For Haemophilus influenzae type b the difference in percentage between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at 1.0 μg/mL threshold was calculated.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For diphtheria toxoid the difference in percentage between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at 0.1 IU/mL threshold was calculated.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For Pertussis FHA the difference in percentage between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at 40.5 EU/mL threshold was calculated.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For Pertussis PT the difference in percentage between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at 16.5 EU/mL threshold was calculated.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For Pertussis PRN the difference in percentage between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at 26 EU/mL threshold was calculated.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) was \> -10%.

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Achieving Predefined Antibody Levels for Concomitant Vaccine Antigens Induced by Measles, Mumps, Rubella, Varicella (MMR-V) and Haemophilus Influenzae Type b (Hib)
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Evaluable immunogenicity (per protocol) population of eligible participants who adhered to the protocol requirements, had valid and determinate assay results, and had no other major protocol violations.(n)=number of participants with a determinate posttoddler dose antibody concentration to the given concomitant antigen.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: One month after toddler dose (13 to 16 months of age)

groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) \> -10%.

analyses.groupDescription: For Measles the difference in percentage between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at ≥1.10 I.V. threshold was calculated.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) \> -5%.

analyses.groupDescription: For Mumps the difference in percentage between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at ≥1.10 I.V. threshold was calculated.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) \> -5%.

analyses.groupDescription: For Rubella the difference in percentage between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at ≥15 IU/mL threshold was calculated.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) \> -5%.

analyses.groupDescription: For Varicella the difference in percentage between the two groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) at ≥1.09 I.V. threshold was calculated.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lowest limit of 2-sided 95% CI for the difference between the 2 groups (13vPnC - 7vPnC) \> -10%.

outcomeMeasures.title: Geometric Mean Antibody Concentration of Hib PRP in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the Toddler Dose
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: The all-available toddler immunogenicity population included all subjects who had at least 1 valid and determinate assay result before (excluding postinfant) or after the toddler dose. N = number of participants with a determinate antibody concentration or index value for the specified concomitant antigen.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: one month after the toddler dose

groups.title: 13vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13vPnC coadministered with measles, mumps, rubella varicella vaccine live (ProQuad), Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Meningococcal Protein Conjugate) (PedvaxHIB), and Hepatitis A Vaccine, Inactivated (VAQTA) at 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 7vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (7vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2, 4, 6 months (infant series). Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 7vPnC coadministered with measles, mumps, rubella varicella vaccine live (ProQuad), Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (Meningococcal Protein Conjugate) (PedvaxHIB), and Hepatitis A Vaccine, Inactivated (VAQTA) at 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

analyses.groupDescription: For Haemophilus influenzae type b (PRP) the GMC ratio (13vPnC/7vPnC) was calculated
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority for concomitant antigens was declared if the lower bound of the 2-sided, 95% CI for the GMC ratio (13vPnC group/7vPnC group) was \>0.5 (2-fold criterion).

outcomeMeasures.title: Geometric Mean Antibody Concentration of Measles, Mumps, and Varicella ELISA in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the Toddler Dose
outcomeMeasures.description: Normalization was performed for unit of measure "index value" as Index Value of 1.00 = 10 mIU/mL.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: The all-available toddler immunogenicity population included all subjects who had at least 1 valid and determinate assay result before (excluding postinfant) or after the toddler dose. N = number of participants with a determinate antibody concentration or index value for the specified concomitant antigen.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: one month after the toddler dose

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-specified Systemic Events
outcomeMeasures.description: Systemic events (any fever \[Fv\] ≥ 38 degrees Celsius \[C\], decreased (decr.) appetite, irritability, increased (incr.) sleep, decreased sleep, and hives \[urticaria\], use of antipyretic medication \[med\] to treat or prevent symptoms \[sx\]) were reported using an electronic diary. Participants may be represented in more than 1 category.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Safety population who received the given vaccination. (n)= number of participants reporting yes for at least 1 day or no for all days.

groups.title: 13vPnC Dose 1
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5 mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) coadministered with Haemophilus b Conjugate Vaccine (ActHIB) and Diphtheria and Tetanus Toxoids and Acellular Pertussis Adsorbed, Hepatitis B (Recombinant) and Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine Combined (Pediarix) at 2 months (infant series).

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-specified Local Reactions
outcomeMeasures.description: Local reactions were reported using an electronic diary. Tenderness was scaled as Any (tenderness present); Significant (\[Sig.\], present and interfered with limb movement). Redness and swelling were scaled as Any (redness or swelling present); Mild (0.5 centimeters \[cm\] to 2.0 cm); Moderate (\[Mod.\], 2.5 to 7.0 cm); Severe (\[Sev.\], \> 7.0 cm). Participants may have been represented in more than 1 category.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Safety population who received the given vaccination. (n)= number of participants reporting yes for at least 1 day or no for all days.

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Achieving Functional Antibody Titer ≥1:8 as Measured by Opsonophagocytic Activity Assay (OPA) in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group the 3-Dose Infant Series and the Toddler Dose
outcomeMeasures.description: Percentage of participants achieving functional antibody titer ≥1:8 as measured by opsonophagocytic activity assay (OPA) along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Evaluable immunogenicity (per protocol) population of eligible participants who adhered to the protocol requirements, had valid and determinate assay results, and had no other major protocol violations;(n) = number of participants with a determinate postinfant series OPA antibody titer to the given serotype.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: One month after infant series and one month after toddler dose

groups.title: 13vPnC After Infant Series
groups.description: Participants received 1 single 0.5 mL dose together with a concomitant dose of Pediarix and ActHIB at the 2-, 4-, and 6-month visits and ProQuad, PedvaxHIB, and VAQTA at the 12-15 month visit.

groups.title: 7vPnC After Infant Series
groups.description: Participants received 1 single 0.5 mL dose together with a concomitant dose of Pediarix and ActHIB at the 2-, 4-, and 6-month visits and ProQuad, PedvaxHIB, and VAQTA at the 12-15 month visit.

groups.title: 13vPnC After Toddler Dose
groups.description: Participants received 1 single 0.5 mL dose together with a concomitant dose of Pediarix and ActHIB at the 2-, 4-, and 6-month visits and ProQuad, PedvaxHIB, and VAQTA at the 12-15 month visit.

groups.description: Participants received 1 single 0.5 mL dose together with a concomitant dose of Pediarix and ActHIB at the 2-, 4-, and 6-month visits and ProQuad, PedvaxHIB, and VAQTA at the 12-15 month visit.

outcomeMeasures.title: Geometric Mean Titer (GMT) as Measured by Opsonophagocytic Activity Assay (OPA) in 13vPnC Group Relative to 7vPnC Group After the 3-Dose Infant Series and the Toddler Dose
outcomeMeasures.description: Geometric mean titer (GMT) as measured by opsonophagocytic activity assay (OPA) for 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Evaluable immunogenicity (per protocol) population of eligible subjects who adhered to the protocol requirements, had valid and determinate assay results, and had no other major protocol violations, (n) = number of participants with a determinate antibody titer for the specified serotype.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: one month after the infant series and the toddler dose

certainAgreement.otherDetails: Pfizer has the right to review disclosures, requesting a delay of less than 60 days. Investigator will postpone single center publications until after disclosure of pooled data (all sites), less than 12 months from study completion/termination at all participating sites. Investigator may not disclose previously undisclosed confidential information other than study results.
